info.title: MAB API
info.description:
The multiple authentication broker (MAB) platform is a new product launched by Swisscom Trust Services in 2023 
and enables electronic signature capabilities (AES and QES) supporting various authentication and identification methods. 
The service consists of a set of Application Programming Interface (APIs) based on OpenID Connect OIDC and 
European Telecommunications Standards Institute (ETSI) compliant with eIDAS (Electronic Identification, 
Authentication and Trust Services) or ZertES (Zertifizierungsdienste im Bereich der elektronischen Signatur) regulations.

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

ident_method_type.description: Defines which ident method should be used. Required for scope ident.

ident_method_delegated_account_id.description: Optional, defines the id of the delegated account to be used.

ident_method_success_redirect_uri.description: optionally for fidentity ident method, the URI to which the ident provider shall redirect the user after successful ident

ident_method_error_redirect_uri.description: optionally for fidentity ident method, the URI to which the ident provider shall redirect the user on error

tac_hash_zertes.description: Required for scope ident and jurisdiction ZertES. Valid hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the /api/auth/realms/broker/.well-known/openid-configuration.

tac_hash_eidas.description: Required for scope ident and jurisdiction eIDAS. Valid hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the Configuration Endpoint.

claims_token.description:
Required for scope sign. This is a JWT token containing the following claims:
credentialID (optional): The credential ID associated with the signing level. Enum values: [OnDemand-Advanced4, OnDemand-Advanced4.1-EU, OnDemand-Qualified4, OnDemand-Qualified4.1-EU] example: OnDemand-Advanced4,
documentDigests (optional): Mandatory only when scope = sign. When scope = no_initial_hash, this field must be omitted.
{
hash: hash of the document,
label (optional): a label for the document
},
hashAlgorithmOID (optional): Enum values: [2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.1, 2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.2, 2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.3],
numSignatures (optional): Mandatory only for scope no_initial_hash. Number of documents that will be signed.

login_hint_token.description:
This is a JWT token containing the following claims, identifier is optional for scope enroll: identifier (optional): desired authentication identifier,
namespace: desired authentication method type Enum values: [msisdn, MSA, STS, MSAINT, FUT, PWDOTP, WBAUTHN],
email (optional): email that will be used by MAB to communicate to the user in case of need

scope.description: multiple scopes can be defined using a space separator

token_delivery_mode.description: Defines the way the token is delivered to the client: pull means the client has to pull until the result is available, push means that the initiating client is retrieving a callback as soon as the result is ready, for this the callback url has to be defined in the oidc client configuration.

callback_url.description: If token_delivery_mode 'push' or 'ping' is selected, this parameter is mandatory and the callback is sent to this url. The url has to be whitelisted in MAB, additional parameters may be added to the url.

client_notification_token.description: If token_delivery_mode 'push' or 'ping' is selected, this parameter is mandatory. Bearer token provided by the client that will be sent back in the header of the callback request for the client to authenticate the callback. The length of the token MUST NOT exceed 1024 characters and it MUST conform to the syntax for Bearer credentials.

auth_req_id.description: the auth request id that is needed to get the token via the oauth-token api

expires_in.description: The number of seconds for how long the authentication request will be valid.

mobile_uri.description: on mobile devices use this uri instead of the qr_code

session_id.description: the session id of the auth provider

qr_code.description: the QR code, in format data:image/png;base64

ident_process_id.description: For scope ident, this parameter contains the process id of the ident process

ident_process_qr_code.description: For scope ident, this parameter contains the QR code of the ident process. It is the qr code of the ident_process_data content.

ident_process_data.description: For scope ident, this parameter contains the data of the ident process. For TI&M it is the app deeplink. For sumsub-video it is the websdk access token.

id_token.description:
contains the following claims:
exp: token expiration date,
iat: time of token generation,
iss: issuer,
aud: oidc client id,
sub: user id,
device_id: device id,
transaction_id: transaction id,
auth_req_id (optional): auth_req_id,
state (optional): initial request state,
namespace: evidence namespace

auth_req_id (optional): auth_req_id,
state (optional): initial request state,
namespace: evidence namespace

access_token.description: This field holds an intermediate access token, which must be exchanged for a SAD through the Token API using grant_type=token-exchange. See the token operation for more information.

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

redirect_uri.description:
Mandatory for CSC: The URL where the user was redirected after the
authorization process completed. It is used to validate
that it matches the original value previously passed to the
authorization server. This SHALL be used only if the
redirect_uri parameter was included in the authorization
request, and their values SHALL be identical.

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

scope.description: The scope, multiple scopes can be defined using a space separator

seal_name.description: optional value. Must be provided for signing with seal (static values for the claims credentialID field)

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

token.description: The token to revoke, this can either be an access or refresh token. If the token passed to the request is a refresh_token, then the authorization server invalidates the refresh token and all access tokens based on the same authorization grant. If the token passed to the request is an access_token, then the authorization server invalidates just the access token.

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

subject_token.description: A security token that represents the identity of the party on behalf of whom the request is being made. Typically, the subject of this token will be the subject of the security token issued in response to the request.

subject_token_type.description: An identifier that indicates the type of the security token in the subject_token parameter.

requested_token_type.description: An identifier that indicates the type of the security token requested. If this parameter is not present, the authorization server MAY issue an access token or a security token of another type, at its discretion.

client_id.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

client_secret.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

redirect_uri.description: the url to which the application will redirect back after processing

email.description: email that will be used by MAB to communicate to the user in case of need

login_hint.description: Used to preselected the authentication method (namespace) for the user. If not provided, an auth method selection page is shown to the user.

state.description: optional value. The state parameter to correlate the original request and response, it will be added as the 'state' parameter in the redirect back call

client_session_id.description: optional value that will can be be used to show a custom state to show to the user in the screens

scope.description: multiple scopes can be defined using a space separator

credentialID.description: The credential ID associated with the signing level.

items.description: Mandatory only when scope = sign. When scope = no_initial_hash, this field must be omitted.

numSignatures.description: Mandatory only for scope no_initial_hash. Number of documents that will be signed.

delegated_account_id.description: If a customer specific ident method account shall be used, the corresponding uuid has to be defined here. The uuid is provided by the support team.

ident_methods.description: defines which ident methods should be shown to the user to chose, if only 1 is defined the selection is skipped

user_email.description: if passed the email will be used to communicate with the client if needed

hash_zertes.description: Valid hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the /api/auth/realms/broker/.well-known/openid-configuration.

hash_eidas.description: Valid hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the /api/auth/realms/broker/.well-known/openid-configuration.

terms_and_conditions.description: optional value. If passed the t&c will considered as approved. Hence the t&c screen will no not be shown. Valid hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the /api/auth/realms/broker/.well-known/openid-configuration

seal_name.description: optional value. Must be provided for signing with seal (static values for the claims credentialID field)

expires_in.description: the validity of the par request

request_uri.description: the request_uri that shall be used in the authentication call

authenticated.description: Indicates if the OTP verification was successful.

reSendingAllowed.description: Number of remaining OTP resend attempts.

authenticated.description: Indicates if the OTP verification was successful.

PwdOtpParRequestExample.summary: This is an example of the PAR request with the password and OTP/SMS authentication method ("PWDOTP" namespace).

MobileIdParRequestExample.summary: This is an example of the PAR request with MobileID authentication method (namespace "msisdn)".

TacProvidedRequestExample.summary: This is an example of the PAR request with terms and conditions provided by the client ("tac_provided" scope).

terms_and_conditions.hash_zertes: to replace by valid hash for zertes. Hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the /api/auth/realms/broker/.well-known/openid-configuration
terms_and_conditions.hash_eidas: to replace by valid hash for eidas. Hashes are returned from the terms_and_conditions_endpoint api defined in the /api/auth/realms/broker/.well-known/openid-configuration

get.description: The OpenID Configuration API provides metadata about an OpenID Connect provider, including URLs for authorization, token, and user info endpoints, supported scopes, grant types, and public key details. It simplifies client integration by automating discovery.
The OpenID Certs API provides the public JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) used to verify JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) issued by the OpenID provider. It ensures secure token validation for client applications.
The starting point for the browser-based ETSI authorization flow.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

parameters.name: request_uri
parameters.description: Optional, the code obtained from the previous PAR request.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The redirect url. This parameter is only used if not defined in the PAR request.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The scope, multiple scopes can be defined using a space separator. This parameter is only used if not defined in the PAR request. Valid scopes are sign,enroll,auth,ident

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The state parameter to correlate the original request and response. This parameter is only used if not defined in the PAR request.

parameters.name: nonce
parameters.description: The nonce value is added to the access token (UUID).

parameters.name: claims
parameters.description: URL encoded claims. This parameter is only used if not defined in the PAR request.

get.description: The starting point for the browser-based CSC authorization flow.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The redirect url.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The scope. Valid scopes are service,credential

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The state parameter to correlate the original request and response.

parameters.name: nonce
parameters.description: The nonce value is added to the access token (UUID).

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: The response type.

parameters.name: credentialID
parameters.description: The credential Id. Mandatory for scope credential.

parameters.name: numSignatures
parameters.description: The number of signatures. Mandatory for scope credential.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: The document hashes separated by comma. Only supported for scope credential.

parameters.name: account_token
parameters.description: The account token, containing information about the signature application and signed with the sha256 hash of the client_secret UUID provided by the support team.

post.description:
THIS API IS PROTECTED BY MTLS, THE CORRESPONDING CERTIFICATE IS PROVIDED BY THE SUPPORT TEAM.

The Pushed Authorization Request (PAR) is specification that allows to secure the OIDC authorization flow. Instead of providing most of the parameter in the authorization url, the PAR request allows to pass these parameters via a back-channel call secured with mTLS. The login_hint parameter can be passed to this interface, so that nobody can change its payload or value in the authorization call. The following generic curl request below show what kind of parameters can be passed to the PAR request

FOR NON-CSC FLOWS, THIS API IS PROTECTED BY MTLS, THE CORRESPONDING CERTIFICATE IS PROVIDED BY THE SUPPORT TEAM.

Returns the SAD token, that can be used to sign the document. Based on the clients configuration it also returns a refresh token that can be used to refresh to SAD token.

THIS API IS PROTECTED BY MTLS, THE CORRESPONDING CERTIFICATE IS PROVIDED BY THE SUPPORT TEAM.

CIBA allows a signature application to start an electronic signature with a back-channel call instead of a browser based flow.

post.summary: Ping callback triggered after processing of request

get.description:
THIS API IS PROTECTED BY MTLS, THE CORRESPONDING CERTIFICATE IS PROVIDED BY THE SUPPORT TEAM.


Webfinger to discover information about resources. In particular, it is used to check if a user is eligible to sign a document using a given credentialID.

If the resource is not existing, expect a 404 response.

The following table describes the different serial mismatch scenarios. For more information, please refer to the [serial mismatch](https://trustservices.swisscom.com/en/support/help-center/what-does-serial-number-mismatch-mean):

| Status Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `MISMATCH_SAS_BUT_MID` | You have activated MID after you were registered with PWD OTP |
| `MISMATCH_SAS_CHANGE_SN` | You have changed your Password after your Identification |
| `MISMATCH_MID` | You have re-activated MID without recovery code after you were registered with MID |

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The client identifier UUID provided by the support team

parameters.name: client_secret
parameters.description: The client secret UUID provided by the support team

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: resource identifier, format urn:mab:acct:identifier:namespace

parameters.name: credentialID
parameters.description: credentialID for which we want to check eligibility

With CredentialID and namespace msisdn.summary: With `credentialID` for `msisdn` namespace, the resulting platform can be either `MID` or `SAS`. The platform property is `msisdn` namespace specific only.

With CredentialID and other namespaces.summary: With provided `credentialID` for all namespaces except `msisdn`. Keep in mind, there is no platform property.

Evidence validity is less than 90 days.summary: All namespaces with a `credentialID` will include the evidence expiry date when the validity is less than 90 days.

Without credentialID and namespace msisd.summary: Without provided `credentialID` for `msisdn` namespace. There is no `eligible` property.

Without credentialID, all namespaces exc.summary: Without provided `credentialID` for all namespaces except `msisdn`. There is no `eligible` property.
Without provided `credentialID` for all namespaces except `msisdn`, when the evidence validity is less than 90 days.

Serial mismatch cases.summary: Serial mismatch cases can be one of `MISMATCH_SAS_BUT_MID`, `MISMATCH_SAS_CHANGE_SN`, `MISMATCH_MID` - see description for more details.

post.description:
THIS API IS PROTECTED BY MTLS. THE CORRESPONDING CERTIFICATE IS PROVIDED BY THE SUPPORT TEAM.

This endpoint handles OTP (One-Time Password) validation and resending for a client-initiated backchannel authentication flow. It is required as second step for Qualified signatures (after password is provided via /api/pwd-otp-auth/verify-pwd) and as first step for Advanced signatures.

Provide either 'otp' for authentication or 'resend' to resend the OTP SMS (mutually exclusive). Users can resend the OTP up to 3 times. If too many wrong OTPs are submitted (5 attempts), the session is declined. OTP codes expire after 8 minutes from when they were sent. Resending is subject to a 120-second cooldown.

authenticated.description: Indicates if the user is authenticated.

reSendingAllowed.description: Number of remaining OTP resend attempts. User is only allowed to re-send code 3 times.

400.description: Bad request due to invalid input or conditions.

401.description: Unauthorized access due to invalid client credentials.

post.description:
THIS API IS PROTECTED BY MTLS. THE CORRESPONDING CERTIFICATE IS PROVIDED BY THE SUPPORT TEAM.

This endpoint validates the user's password for qualified signatures. On success, an OTP is sent to the user's phone number and the client must proceed with /api/pwd-otp-auth/verify-otp to complete the flow.

400.description: Bad Request due to client errors such as validation failures.

405.description: Method Not Allowed, if the method is not POST.
